checks.a_analysis: The output document faithfully transcribes the headings and paragraphs from the source PDF. No fabricated sections or invented text were found; the content matches the flow and structure of the original 6-page informational document.

While most contact information, including URLs and phone numbers, is correct, there is a critical discrepancy in the TTY number provided in the section regarding keeping a current PCP. On page 3 of the source, the TTY number is listed as 1-833-870-5588. The JSON output incorrectly lists it as 1-833-870-5558. This error matches a typo in the provided OCR but fails the check against the authoritative PDF source.
checks.b_evidence_location: Section: ¿Puedo conservar mi proveedor personal (PCP) actual?, Page 3, list element index 1

checks.a_analysis: All six pages of the source document are accounted for in the JSON output. Every major heading and corresponding body text from the original document is present.
The source document does not contain any tables; it is structured as text blocks and lists. Therefore, this check is not applicable.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output preserves the substantial content of each section. Lists (such as the 5 health plans and the disclaimer about counties) are transcribed in full without missing items or significant truncation.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Other than the critical phone number typo noted in C2, the wording matches the source document's meaning and Spanish terminology accurately. Minor differences in formatting or the inclusion of metadata are within acceptable bounds.

checks.a_analysis: The output JSON was carefully reviewed against the 6-page source document. All repeated information in the JSON (such as contact information and disclaimers) corresponds directly to intentional repetitions within the source document (e.g., the footer-like contact block appearing on multiple pages). No unauthorized content duplication was found.

The document title 'OBTENGA RESPUESTAS' is correctly identified as the primary title in metadata. All subsequent section questions throughout the 6 pages are consistently tagged as Level 2 headings (H2). There are no skipped levels or incorrect nestings. This hierarchy provides logical navigation for screen readers.
There are no tables present in the source document. Consequently, no table structure corruption can occur.

The reading order in the JSON follows the logical top-to-bottom, page-by-page flow of the source document. Information is presented in the sequence intended by the document designer, including the transition between pages (e.g., the flow from the bottom of Page 1 to the top of Page 2).

All lists in the document have been correctly identified and structured. The numbered list on Page 2 ('¿Qué debo hacer?') is correctly rendered as an ordered list. Bulleted lists on Pages 3, 5, and 6 are correctly rendered as unordered lists. The list of health plans on Page 4 is also correctly identified as an ordered list.

All government URLs and contact links found in the source PDF are present in the JSON. While some are rendered as plain text within paragraphs and others as explicit link objects (e.g., in Sections 15, 16, 17, 19), no links from the source are missing, broken, or fabricated.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document correctly adapts the content from the 6-page PDF into a single-column web layout. The logical flow of information is maintained, with headings and paragraphs appearing in the same relative order as the source. While the main title 'OBTENGA RESPUESTAS' has been moved to the very top of the document (above the introductory text that originally preceded it on Page 1), this change improves the structural hierarchy for web navigation and does not represent a major layout failure. Information remains easy to find.
The source document does not contain any tables. Therefore, this check is not applicable and is passed.

The meaningful image in the source document is the NC Department of Health and Human Services logo/seal, which appears on Page 1 and Page 6. This logo is correctly included in the HTML output at the bottom of the page. Its absence from the header area is a common adaptation for accessibility and document flow, and since the branding is present in the document, this check passes.

There is no evidence of body content being duplicated in the HTML output. Each section and paragraph from the 6-page PDF appears exactly once in the body of the HTML. The inclusion of section headings in the Table of Contents at the top is a standard accessibility feature and does not constitute content duplication.
